mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real GDP (GDPC1) over one quarter signals an economic contraction that reduces corporate profits and investor confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is negative but SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances, the hypothesis will be rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, it confirms a deteriorating labor market that weakens consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after trigger activation (UNRATE > 5.5) the S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive in more than 40% of cases, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous occurrence of high unemployment (UNRATE > 5%) and very low housing starts (HOUST < 1000) indicates a severe housing-led recession that depresses equity valuations via falling household wealth and credit contraction, driving negative forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition holds but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 30% of triggered instances, the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) falls below its 12-month moving average, any additional decline in INDPRO amplifies the negative impact on equities as the manufacturing recession deepens, especially when the Fed has limited room to cut rates (FEDFUNDS already low). This interaction predicts negative forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: If the condition INDPRO < 12-month MA holds but subsequent INDPRO declines are associated with positive S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of cases, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) erode the real value of future corporate cash flows and reduce purchasing power, leading to lower equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. This effect is persistent across economic regimes.
failure_condition: If the monthly change in CPIAUCSL is positive but the S&P 500 20-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) of more than 1% signals an immediate drop in consumer spending, which is a leading indicator of economic weakness that will negatively impact corporate profits and equity prices within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the trigger condition is met and the S&P 500 5-day forward return is positive more than 50% of the time, the hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: In a severe recession with unemployment above 6% and the federal funds rate already cut below 2%, further monetary easing fails to stimulate equity markets because the transmission mechanism is impaired (liquidity trap), leading to negative forward returns over the next 60 trading days as the recession deepens.
failure_condition: If the trigger condition holds and the S&P 500 60-day forward return is positive in more than 30% of cases, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of low inflation (CPIAUCSL below 220), increases in industrial production (INDPRO) signal a strengthening economy without overheating, which boosts corporate earnings and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. This relationship reverses under high inflation.
failure_condition: If in periods where CPIAUCSL < 220 and INDPRO is rising, the S&P 500 20-day forward return is negative more than 60% of the time, the hypothesis is rejected.
